config_files.notes: Fortnite uses WindowsClient as the config subdirectory, not WindowsNoEditor. Overwritten on every game exit. Edit only while Fortnite is fully closed.
Engine-level overrides. Not overwritten by in-game settings. Can be used to disable motion blur and other post-process effects persistently.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Fortnite uses EasyAntiCheat. Config file edits are permitted. Certain CVar injections at launch are monitored. All keys in this profile are standard config-file entries and EAC-safe.
engine_overrides.action: Safe to apply all recommended settings.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Fortnite uses UE5 Nanite geometry and Lumen global illumination. Some UE5-standard Engine.ini keys for disabling Lumen (r.Lumen.Reflections.Allow=0, r.Lumen.DiffuseIndirect.Allow=0) are blocked server-side in Fortnite Battle Royale — the game re-enables them via server-pushed config.
engine_overrides.action: Do not recommend r.Lumen.* disable keys for Fortnite. Instead direct users to the in-game Rendering Mode selector.

keys.effect: Render resolution scale as a percentage. Range 25-200.
keys.notes: Fortnite supports DLSS, FSR, and TSR natively. When any upscaler is enabled in-game, sg.ResolutionQuality is overridden by the upscaler's internal resolution ratio. Only applies meaningfully when using native rendering.

keys.effect: Post-processing quality including bloom, lens flare, and ambient occlusion: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high, 3=epic.

keys.notes: Fortnite's UE5 particle system can cause significant frame time spikes at Epic during heavy storm/build sequences. Medium is a good quality/performance balance.

keys.effect: View distance for terrain, objects and characters: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high, 3=epic.
keys.notes: Competitive players keep this at Epic (3) — reduced view distance means enemies further than the threshold are invisible, which is a significant gameplay disadvantage.

engine_ini_keys.notes: Disabled by default in Fortnite via in-game setting. Adding to Engine.ini ensures it cannot be accidentally re-enabled after a patch reset.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Performance Mode (DirectX 11) on low/mid hardware for maximum FPS. DirectX 12 on high-end for Nanite/Lumen. DirectX 11 Performance Mode bypasses UE5 features and runs on a simplified renderer.
manual_only_settings.reason: Rendering mode selection is in-game only and fundamentally changes the render pipeline.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: DLSS Quality on RTX hardware. FSR Quality on AMD. TSR (Epic's native) as fallback. Performance Mode users cannot use TSR.

manual_only_settings.location: Settings > Video > NVIDIA Reflex Low Latency
manual_only_settings.recommendation: On + Boost for all NVIDIA GPU owners.

notes: Fortnite's config path uses WindowsClient, not WindowsNoEditor. This is a common mistake when applying generic UE4/UE5 guides.

Fortnite Performance Mode (DX11) is a completely separate renderer from the default DX12 mode. Performance Mode disables Nanite, Lumen, and most UE5 features in exchange for significantly higher FPS on low/mid hardware. Recommend explicitly for hardware tiers below mid.
Epic Games periodically resets graphics settings to defaults with major seasonal updates. Advise users to re-check settings after each new Fortnite chapter.
Fortnite is available only via the Epic Games Store — not on Steam. The config path reflects this (no Steam AppID).
